D10 Imaging L6 Create Reference

Night Mode — Burst Averaging Reduces Noise by √N

capture N short-exposure frames and average them per pixel; both Poisson shot noise and Gaussian read noise drop by √N — that's how phones photograph the dark

A single long exposure suffers handshake and saturation. A burst of N short exposures, aligned and averaged per pixel, gets the same total light with √N less noise. Hasinoff et al. 2016 (Google HDR+) showed this on phone-class sensors; Apple Night Mode and Pixel Night Sight ship the same recipe with motion-tolerant alignment.

The left panel shows a single noisy short-exposure capture. The right panel shows the average of N captures of the same scene. Drag N from 1 to 16 — the right panel smooths out, the readout shows σ dropping as 1/√N.
